description: Basic overflow vulnerability confirmed in test contract. The testBasicFunctionality() demonstrates an arithmetic overflow when adding 1 to maxUint256, causing a panic revert. This suggests the codebase may be vulnerable to similar overflow/underflow issues in financial calculations.
logs: [FAIL: panic: arithmetic underflow or overflow (0x11)] testBasicFunctionality() (gas: 974)

description: Critical overflow vulnerability detected in amount manipulation test. The testAmountManipulation() function fails with panic: arithmetic underflow or overflow (0x11) when testing with maximum uint256 value (115792089237316195423570985008687907853269984665640564039457584007913129639935). This indicates the minting contract is vulnerable to overflow attacks when processing large amounts, potentially allowing attackers to mint tokens with insufficient collateral.
logs: [FAIL: panic: arithmetic underflow or overflow (0x11)] testAmountManipulation() (gas: 13691)

description: Staking attack simulations completed successfully. All 4 tests passed, indicating that basic staking manipulation patterns (share price manipulation, deposit/withdrawal race conditions, slippage manipulation, and max function edge cases) work as expected without immediate vulnerabilities. However, the tests revealed that share conversion calculations remain accurate across edge cases including maximum uint256 values, suggesting proper handling of large numbers in the mock implementation.
logs: Suite result: ok. 4 passed; 0 failed; 0 skipped
Round trip accurate true (for all test cases including max uint256)
